nodes.label: AI as debugging and research tool
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI mostly for debugging code

nodes.grounding_span: I will sometimes use AI to write boilerplate code

nodes.label: AI as collaborative iterative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I'm much more likely to collaborate throughout a task, taking an iterative approach

nodes.label: Code ownership and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: it is much harder for me to debug and improve upon code that I haven't written myself

nodes.label: AI as pragmatic tool without emotional attachment
nodes.grounding_span: I honestly don't really assign much emotion to the outputs of AI

nodes.label: Proactive AI skill adoption

nodes.grounding_span: it has essentially replaced the process I would previously follow

nodes.label: Competence and control over work
nodes.grounding_span: it is much harder for me to debug and improve upon code that I haven't written myself

nodes.label: Pragmatic adaptability
nodes.grounding_span: if it works it works, if it doesn't, either I have to fix it with better prompting

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for debugging and research
nodes.grounding_span: it has essentially replaced the process I would previously follow

nodes.label: Preference for manual complex coding
nodes.grounding_span: I do not like to do this, as it is much harder for me to debug and improve upon code that I haven't written myself

nodes.label: Emotional neutrality toward AI
nodes.grounding_span: I honestly don't really assign much emotion to the outputs of AI

nodes.label: Iterative guided problem-solving

nodes.label: Pragmatic outcome-focused evaluation
